symptoms.description: Machine does not spin during final cycle
Water remains in drum after cycle completes
Error code E21 displayed on panel

failure_modes.name: Worn Drive Belt
failure_modes.description: Drive belt has stretched or snapped, preventing drum rotation.
failure_modes.safety_notes: Disconnect power before removing rear panel. Belt tension can snap fingers.

failure_modes.name: Failed Drain Pump
failure_modes.description: Pump impeller blocked or motor burned out, preventing water evacuation.
failure_modes.safety_notes: Residual water may leak when opening filter. Place towels and unplug unit.

failure_modes.name: Unbalanced Load Sensor Fault
failure_modes.description: Suspension rods or shock absorbers degraded, triggering imbalance shutdown.
failure_modes.safety_notes: Do not bypass safety sensor. Machine may walk across floor if forced to spin.

diagnostic_steps.description: Run empty spin cycle and listen for motor engagement.
diagnostic_steps.expected_outcome: Motor hums but drum does not rotate

diagnostic_steps.description: Inspect drive belt through rear access panel.
Check drain filter and run pump test mode.
diagnostic_steps.expected_outcome: Pump does not activate or makes grinding noise

diagnostic_steps.description: Inspect suspension rods for play and oil leakage.
diagnostic_steps.expected_outcome: Rods loose or shocks leaking fluid

historical_resolutions.description: Replaced worn drive belt; spin restored.
historical_resolutions.technician_notes: Customer reported 6 months of intermittent spin failure. Belt showed heat cracking.

historical_resolutions.description: Cleared coin blockage and replaced drain pump.
historical_resolutions.technician_notes: Coin jam caused pump burnout. Recommended pocket-check habit.

symptoms.description: Dishes come out wet and cold
Standing water at bottom after cycle

failure_modes.name: Heating Element Failure
failure_modes.description: Heating element open circuit; water does not reach sanitizing temperature.
failure_modes.safety_notes: Element remains hot briefly after cycle. Allow 30 min cool-down before service.

failure_modes.name: Clogged Drain Hose
failure_modes.description: Kinked or blocked drain hose prevents complete drainage.
failure_modes.safety_notes: Water in sump may contain food debris. Wear gloves.

failure_modes.name: Wash Impeller Obstruction
failure_modes.description: Glass shard or bone fragment jamming circulation pump impeller.
failure_modes.safety_notes: Sharp debris risk. Disconnect power and water supply first.

diagnostic_steps.description: Run rinse-only cycle and measure outlet water temperature.
Inspect drain hose for kinks behind kick plate.
diagnostic_steps.expected_outcome: Hose pinched or filled with grease buildup

diagnostic_steps.description: Remove lower spray arm and check impeller chamber.
diagnostic_steps.expected_outcome: Foreign object visible or impeller does not spin freely

diagnostic_steps.description: Test detergent dispenser solenoid with multimeter.
diagnostic_steps.expected_outcome: No continuity on wax motor solenoid

historical_resolutions.description: Replaced heating element; sanitizing temperature restored.
historical_resolutions.technician_notes: Hard water scale contributed to element burnout. Advised monthly descale.

historical_resolutions.description: Removed glass fragment from impeller; noise eliminated.
historical_resolutions.technician_notes: Customer loaded chipped glass. Recommended pre-rinse inspection.

symptoms.description: Microwave runs but food stays cold

failure_modes.name: Magnetron Failure
failure_modes.description: Magnetron filament or high-voltage diode failed; no microwave energy produced.
failure_modes.safety_notes: HIGH VOLTAGE. Capacitor retains charge. Only certified technicians should service.

failure_modes.name: Damaged Waveguide Cover
failure_modes.description: Grease buildup on mica waveguide cover causing arcing and burn-through.
failure_modes.safety_notes: Do not operate with damaged cover. Arcing can damage magnetron.

failure_modes.name: Convection Fan Motor Failure
failure_modes.description: Fan motor bearing seized; convection bake mode ineffective.
failure_modes.safety_notes: Unit may overheat in convection mode. Stop use until repaired.

diagnostic_steps.description: Heat cup of water for 60 seconds on high power.
Inspect waveguide cover for burn marks or holes.
Run convection bake at 180°C and listen for fan.
Check door switch continuity with door closed.

historical_resolutions.technician_notes: Grease splatter from uncovered dish caused cover damage.

historical_resolutions.description: Replaced magnetron after confirmed HV diode failure.
historical_resolutions.technician_notes: Unit out of warranty. Customer opted for repair over replacement.
